conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
That's good to know. So, what's been the highlight of your week?
Honestly? Nothing too exciting. Just got some work done and then came home and watched some TV.
Okay, sounds like you had a pretty routine week. How about your job as an insurance adjuster? Any new developments or challenges?
Yeah, just the usual stuff. Trying to get through my workload. Sometimes it feels like I'm drowning in papers.
That can be overwhelming. Do you find yourself having any trouble sleeping lately?
Sleep is fine. I mean, it's not great, but I'm getting by.
Okay, so you're managing okay with sleep. What about your energy levels? Are you feeling more tired or sluggish than usual?
Yeah, definitely. Some days I just want to crawl back into bed and take a nap in the middle of the day.
That sounds tough. How's your appetite been doing lately?
Honestly, it's been all over the place. One day I'll be starving and then the next day I won't really feel like eating at all.
Okay, so you're experiencing some changes in your appetite. Have you noticed any changes in your interest or motivation levels as well?
Yeah, it's like I just go through the motions most days. Don't really care about anything anymore.
That sounds really tough. Have you been doing anything you enjoy lately to take your mind off things?
Not really. I mean, sometimes I'll watch a movie or something, but nothing that really gets me excited.
Okay, so it sounds like you're struggling with some motivation and interest. How about your concentration? Are you having trouble focusing on tasks at work or in your personal life?
Yeah, it's like my brain just feels foggy all the time. I'll be trying to do something simple and suddenly I'll forget what I was doing.
Okay, so you're experiencing some fogginess. Have you noticed any physical sensations or body feelings that might be related to this?
Honestly? Just a lot of tension in my shoulders and neck. It feels like I'm just holding everything together by sheer force of will.
That sounds really uncomfortable. What's been going on lately that might be causing some stress for you?
Well, work has been a bit of a nightmare to be honest. We've had some major claims come in and it's been a real struggle to keep up with the workload.
Okay, so it sounds like you're feeling a lot of pressure at work. Have you reached out to anyone for support or talked to friends about what's going on?
Yeah, I mean, my family knows things are tough, but we don't really talk about it that much. And as for friends...I don't know, it feels like they're all busy with their own stuff.
Okay, so you're feeling a bit isolated right now. What do you think your goals are for this session or for moving forward?
Honestly? I just want to feel better about myself and my life again. Like, not be stuck in this rut.
That's a great place to start. Let's work on building some strategies together to help you get there. Is there anything that you've tried so far that seems to have helped even a little bit?
Well, I did try taking a few days off work and just relaxing, but it didn't really do much for me.
Okay, so we can explore some other options together then. What are your thoughts on seeking support from a therapist or counselor?
Honestly? I'm not sure. It feels like I should be able to handle this on my own.

That's completely understandable. But sometimes having someone outside of the situation can offer a fresh perspective and help you find new ways of coping. Would you be open to exploring some resources or referrals?
Yeah, I suppose it couldn't hurt to look into it.

Great! Let's make a plan to follow up on those resources together. And before we wrap up today, is there anything in particular that you're looking forward to or hoping for from this session or from our work together moving forward?
Yeah, just feeling like I can finally get out of bed without having to force myself.
That's a great goal. We'll definitely work on building some strategies to help you with that.
